title: Implement Invite Code System with Welcome Screen

requirements.summary: Create welcome screen with app icon at top and centered layout
acceptanceCriteria: Welcome screen displays app icon/logo at top center
Layout is clean and centered on the page
Visual design follows general pattern from uploaded FamilyHub screenshot

file_operations.description: Create a new welcome screen page component with centered layout, app icon at top (green rounded square with house icon similar to FamilyHub screenshot), welcome message 'Welcome to FamilyHub', and descriptive text explaining the two main actions. Use Tailwind CSS for styling with a clean, centered card-based layout matching the FamilyHub design pattern.

Add routing logic to show WelcomeScreen for authenticated users who have no family/team association. Check user profile after login to determine if they need to see the welcome screen or proceed directly to the main app. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add 'Create a New Family' button that makes user admin/owner
acceptanceCriteria: Button is styled as primary action (green color)
Clicking button creates a new family/team with current user as admin/owner
User is redirected to main app after family creation

file_operations.description: Add a primary green button labeled 'Create a New Family' with subtitle 'Start fresh as the family admin' and a plus icon. Style it prominently as the primary action with hover states. Wire the button to trigger family creation flow using the authorization component's role assignment capabilities.

Add a useCreateFamily mutation hook that calls backend capability to initialize a new family/team and assign the current user as admin. Include success callback to redirect user to the main Dashboard page. Handle error states appropriately.

requirements.summary: Add 'Join Existing Family' button that prompts for invite code
acceptanceCriteria: Button is styled as secondary action (gray color)
Clicking button opens input dialog for entering invite code
After entering valid code, user joins the corresponding family/team
User is redirected to main app after successful join

file_operations.description: Add a secondary gray/neutral button labeled 'Join Existing Family' with subtitle 'Use an invite code from your family' and a link icon. Style it as a secondary action. Wire it to open a dialog component for invite code entry. Use the invite-links component to support validation and code redemption functionality. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a dialog component with an input field for entering the invite code, submit button, cancel button, loading states, and error message display. Use BaseDialog for consistent styling. Include form validation and handle backend validation errors gracefully. Use the invite-links component for code validation. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add a useJoinFamily mutation hook that calls backend capability to validate and use the invite code, then assign the user to the appropriate family/team with the role specified in the invite. Include success callback to redirect to Dashboard. Handle 'code not found' and 'code already used' errors with user-friendly messages.

requirements.summary: Add 'or' text divider between the two main action buttons
acceptanceCriteria: Text 'or' appears centered between the two main buttons
Divider is visually subtle but clearly separates the two options

file_operations.description: Add a centered text divider displaying 'or' between the 'Create a New Family' and 'Join Existing Family' buttons. Style it with subtle gray color and appropriate spacing to visually separate the two primary actions, matching the FamilyHub screenshot design.

requirements.summary: Add logout link at bottom of welcome screen
acceptanceCriteria: Logout link appears at bottom of welcome screen
Clicking logout logs user out via Internet Identity
User returns to login page after logout

file_operations.description: Add a logout link at the bottom of the welcome screen with an icon (arrow-right-from-bracket or similar) and text 'Log out'. Use the useInternetIdentity hook's clear function to handle logout and clear all cached data via queryClient.clear(). Style it as a subtle text link with hover state.

requirements.summary: Show welcome screen to users without family/team association
acceptanceCriteria: Welcome screen is shown after login if user has no family/team association
Users who have already joined or created a family see the main app instead

file_operations.description: Add conditional routing logic that checks if authenticated user has a family/team association (via user profile or dedicated backend capability). If no association exists, render WelcomeScreen. If association exists, render the normal app layout with AppLayout and routes. Handle loading states to prevent flickering between screens. Use the authorization component to check user role and team membership status. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add a useHasFamilyAssociation query hook that fetches whether the current user is associated with any family/team. This could check if the user has a team role assigned or if their profile indicates family membership. Enable the query only when user is authenticated. Return loading, error, and data states for use in App.tsx conditional rendering.
